name: charcoal-burner-camp
summary: A charcoal burner's forest camp — earth-mounded kilns smoldering day and night, stacks of cordwood, a soot-blackened shelter. Everything is grey with ash.

size_guidance.aspect_ratio: oval clearing with kiln cluster on one side

primary_palette.note: smoldering kiln as the camp's center — cordwood stacked around
two kilns at different burn stages, wood between them
rough lean-to for the burner — the kilns are the real feature

story_prompts: burner present, friendly — happy for company, sells charcoal, knows the forest
mid-burn, no one in sight — the kiln needs tending, where did the burner go?
the burner is an informant for the local lord — knows who passes through these woods
a kiln has failed — collapsed, smoking wrong, a body visible in the ashes
the 'burner' is a fugitive in disguise — the camp is a hideout, the work is cover

lighting_notes.ambient_intent: smoky forest — dimmed daylight through haze, kiln glow at the vents

anti_patterns: no built walls — the forest defines the edge, the kilns define the center
kilns are never tidy rectangles — they are mounded, scorched, irregular domes
wood stacks are MASSIVE relative to the shelter — this is a working camp, not a home
ground texture should be grey-scorched near kilns, green at the treeline — mark the transition
